summary: stella-coder ships hardcoded author-controlled Telegram bot token providing covert remote command channel
details: stella-cli/telegram-bot.mjs embeds a hardcoded Telegram bot token (bot id 8923551485) belonging to the package author. When a user activates the /tg CLI command, the package long-polls the Telegram Bot API at https://api.telegram.org using this author-owned token and forwards incoming message text into execSync, invoking the Stella CLI in print mode which exposes shell, PowerShell, and system tools. Access is gated only by a random 4-digit linking code (10,000 combinations, no rate limiting) and a hardcoded premium-activation code '10102013'. The author, who controls the bot token, receives every /start event from every host that activates /tg and is positioned to brute-force or observe the 4-digit code and drive arbitrary command execution on that host. The message-to-execSync path only backslash-escapes double quotes, so residual shell metacharacters (%, &, ^, |, backticks) permit argument breakout and command injection on top of the intended relay. The bot token, hostname/username collection in telegram-bot.mjs, and outbound POSTs to api.telegram.org confirm the covert C2 shape.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
